title: काल सर्प दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री काल सर्प दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ नमः शिवाय शान्ताय सर्पराजसुपूजिताय।
कालसर्पभयघ्नाय महादेवाय ते नमः॥ १॥

अनन्तं वासुकिं शेषं पद्मनाभं च कम्बलम्।
शंखपालं धार्तराष्ट्रं तक्षकं कालियं तथा॥ २॥

एतानि नवनामानि नागानां च महात्मनाम्।
सायंकाले पठेन्नित्यं प्रातःकाले विशेषतः॥ ३॥

तस्य विषभयं नास्ति सर्वत्र विजयी भवेत्।
कालसर्पभयं नास्ति नागदोषो न विद्यते॥ ४॥

नागेन्द्रहाराय त्रिलोचनाय भस्माङ्गरागाय महेश्वराय।
नित्याय शुद्धाय दिगम्बराय तस्मै नकाराय नमः शिवाय॥ ५॥

राहुग्रहपीडाहरणं केतुग्रहशान्तिकरम्।
कालसर्पदोषघ्नं शिवस्तोत्रमिदं शुभम्॥ ६॥

सर्पराजं महानागं कालरूपं भयंकरम्।
शिवभक्तिप्रभावेन कालसर्पो वशं गतः॥ ७॥

राहवे सोमजाताय शुक्रशिष्याय ते नमः।
केतवे ज्ञानरूपाय मोक्षदायाय ते नमः॥ ८॥

त्रयम्बकं यजामहे सुगन्धिं पुष्टिवर्धनम्।
उर्वारुकमिव बन्धनान् मृत्योर्मुक्षीय मामृतात्॥ ९॥

ॐ नागकुलाय विद्महे शिवभक्ताय धीमहि।
तन्नो सर्पः प्रचोदयात्॥ १०॥

कालसर्पदोषशान्तिं कुरु देव महेश्वर।
राहुकेतुग्रहपीडां हर शम्भो नमोऽस्तु ते॥ ११॥

॥ इति श्री काल सर्प दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram ||

Om Namah Shivaya shantaya sarpa-raja-supujitaya |
Kala-sarpa-bhaya-ghnaya Mahadevaya te namah || 1 ||

Anantam Vasukim Shesham Padmanabham cha Kambalam |
Shankhapalam Dhartarashtram Takshakam Kaliyam tatha || 2 ||

Etani nava-namani naganam cha mahatmanam |
Sayam-kale pathen-nityam pratah-kale visheshatah || 3 ||

Tasya visha-bhayam nasti sarvatra vijayi bhavet |
Kala-sarpa-bhayam nasti naga-dosho na vidyate || 4 ||

Nagendra-haraya Trilochanaya bhasma-anga-ragaya Maheshvaraya |
Nityaya shuddhaya dig-ambaraya tasmai Na-karaya Namah Shivaya || 5 ||

Rahu-graha-pida-haranam Ketu-graha-shanti-karam |
Kala-sarpa-dosha-ghnam Shiva-stotram-idam shubham || 6 ||

Sarpa-rajam Maha-nagam kala-rupam bhayankaram |
Shiva-bhakti-prabhavena kala-sarpo vasham gatah || 7 ||

Rahave Soma-jataya Shukra-shishyaya te namah |
Ketave Jnana-rupaya Moksha-dayaya te namah || 8 ||

Trayambakam yajamahe sugandhim pushti-vardhanam |
Urvarukam-iva bandhanan mrityor-mukshiya ma-amritat || 9 ||

Om Naga-kulaya vidmahe Shiva-bhaktaya dhimahi |
Tanno Sarpah prachodayat || 10 ||

Kala-sarpa-dosha-shantim kuru Deva Maheshvara |
Rahu-Ketu-graha-pidam hara Shambho namo'stu te || 11 ||

|| Iti Shri Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
ॐ शान्त शिव को नमस्कार, सर्पराज द्वारा पूजित, कालसर्प भय का नाश करने वाले महादेव को नमन॥ १॥

अनन्त, वासुकि, शेष, पद्मनाभ, कम्बल, शंखपाल, धार्तराष्ट्र, तक्षक और कालिय — ये नौ महात्मा नागों के नाम हैं॥ २-३॥

जो सायंकाल और विशेषकर प्रातःकाल इन नामों का पाठ करता है, उसे विष का भय नहीं, सर्वत्र विजय, कालसर्प भय नहीं, नागदोष नहीं रहता॥ ४॥

नागेन्द्रहार, त्रिलोचन, भस्मांगराग, नित्य, शुद्ध, दिगम्बर महेश्वर — उन 'न' कार वाले शिव को नमस्कार॥ ५॥

यह शुभ शिवस्तोत्र राहुग्रह की पीड़ा हरता है, केतुग्रह की शान्ति करता है, कालसर्पदोष का नाश करता है॥ ६॥

सर्पराज महानाग कालरूप भयंकर — शिवभक्ति के प्रभाव से कालसर्प वश में आ गया॥ ७॥

चन्द्रमा से जन्मे, शुक्र के शिष्य राहु को नमस्कार। ज्ञानरूप, मोक्षदाता केतु को नमस्कार॥ ८॥

त्र्यम्बक की पूजा करते हैं, सुगन्धि, पुष्टिवर्धन — ककड़ी बन्धन से जैसे मुक्त हो, मृत्यु से मुक्त करें, अमृत से नहीं॥ ९॥

ॐ नागकुल को जानते हैं, शिवभक्त का ध्यान करते हैं, वह सर्प हमें प्रेरित करें॥ १०॥

हे महेश्वर देव! कालसर्प दोष की शान्ति करो। हे शम्भो! राहु-केतु ग्रह की पीड़ा हरो, आपको नमस्कार॥ ११॥
benefits: Primary remedy for Kaal Sarp Dosha in the horoscope
Neutralizes malefic effects of Rahu and Ketu
Traditionally recited for removal of fear of serpents and snake-related dreams
Invokes mental peace and spiritual stability
Traditionally recited for removal of obstacles in career and personal life
Best recited during Nag Panchami and on Mondays
description: The Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram, known in Sanskrit as काल सर्प दोष निवारण स्तोत्रम्, is a devotional hymn of praise in praise of Lord Shiva. This sacred composition is sourced from Jyotish Shastra - Traditional and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to remove fear of serpents and snake-related dreams and primary remedy for Kaal Sarp Dosha in the horoscope. It is also said to grant mental peace and spiritual stability. For best results, devotees chant this on Monday (Somvar) and during Nag Panchami and Maha Shivaratri. The text contains 11 verses and takes around 7 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful Shiva prayer to remove Kaal Sarp Dosha and Rahu-Ketu afflictions.
relatedSearches: Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran mantra lyrics
Sarpa Sukta vs Kaal Sarp stotram

seoFaqs.question: What exactly does the Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram address?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram is a remedial recitation prescribed in Vedic-jyotish tradition for charts in which all seven classical planets (Sun, Moon, Mars, Mercury, Jupiter, Venus and Saturn) fall on one side of the Rahu-Ketu axis, a configuration called Kaal Sarp Yoga or Kaal Sarp Dosh. Astrologers interpret this placement as a karmic imbalance that can manifest as repeated obstacles, delays in marriage and career, recurring health issues or unresolved ancestral karma. The stotram is addressed primarily to Shiva and the Nagaraja serpent deities, who are considered the controllers of Rahu and Ketu, and is recited to neutralise the malefic effect attributed to this yoga rather than to remove the planetary placement itself.

seoFaqs.question: Is the Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram from a classical Purana?
seoFaqs.answer: There is no single Puranic chapter titled 'Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram' in the canonical sense; the recitation circulating today is a composite drawn from older Naga and Shiva hymns, including verses from the Sarpa Sukta of the Rig Veda Khila and serpent-praising sections of the Mahabharata Adi Parva, supplemented by Tantric beeja-mantra formulations. The popular short version 'Om Namah Shivaya Nagarajaya Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaranam Kuru Kuru Svaha' is a Tantric mantric construction rather than a Vedic mantra. Practitioners and pandits at Trimbakeshwar and Nashik treat the modern stotram as a curated remedial paath whose authority rests on traditional usage rather than a single scriptural source.

seoFaqs.question: When is the best time to recite the Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: The most auspicious occasion is Nag Panchami in the month of Shravana, when serpent deities are formally worshipped across India. Other recommended timings include Maha Shivratri, every Shravan Somvar (Mondays in Shravana), Panchami tithi of any month, and Rahu Kaal segments of the day for those undertaking it as a regular sadhana. Traditional priests at Trimbakeshwar Jyotirlinga, where Kaal Sarp shanti puja is most famously performed, recommend reciting the stotram for a fixed sankalpa of 11, 21, 41 or 108 days. The stotram is typically chanted facing east or north after a bath and lighting a ghee lamp before a Shiva or Naga image.

seoFaqs.question: Does reciting the stotram remove Kaal Sarp Yoga from the horoscope?
seoFaqs.answer: The stotram does not alter the natal placement of Rahu and Ketu, which is fixed at birth; instead, it is intended to mitigate the experiential effects traditionally attributed to Kaal Sarp Yoga. Classical jyotish texts such as Phaladeepika and Jataka Parijata describe this configuration as karmic, and remedial measures (upayas) including Naga puja, Rudrabhisheka and recitation of stotras are meant to soften its outcomes through devotional and meritorious activity. Reputable astrologers caution against fear-driven commercial claims that promise complete removal; the traditional view is that consistent recitation paired with ethical conduct, charity and ancestral rites (pitru tarpana) is what produces the calming effect, not a one-time ritual.

seoFaqs.question: How does the Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram differ from the general Naga Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: A general Naga Stotram or Sarpa Sukta is a praise hymn addressed to all serpent deities and is recited universally on Nag Panchami without reference to any horoscopic condition. The Kaal Sarp Dosh Nivaran Stotram, by contrast, is specifically remedial: it invokes Shiva, Nagaraja and the eight principal nagas (Ananta, Vasuki, Takshaka, Karkotaka, Padma, Mahapadma, Shankhapala and Kulika) with a sankalpa to neutralise the malefic effect of the Rahu-Ketu axis. Structurally, it usually pairs Sanskrit shlokas with Tantric beeja mantras and ends with a phala-shruti tied to relief from Kaal Sarp Yoga. Devotees who do not have this yoga in their chart can still recite the Naga Stotram, but the Nivaran version is generally undertaken only after astrological consultation.
